mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses statistical significance at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in 65% of observed quarters, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, supply-side bottlenecks force firms to cut output and delay capital expenditures, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in 70% of weeks following negative INDPRO growth, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power outside contraction phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 24-month rolling window or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3 percent, anticipated monetary tightening raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 60% of quarters following CPIAUCSL MoM growth above 0.3, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales signals weakening consumer demand despite aggregate growth, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in 55% of cases, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate environment, further reductions in the federal funds rate provide diminishing marginal stimulus, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week due to growth concerns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in 70% of rate cuts during the zero lower bound regime, or the negative signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.
